notes: llama.cpp baselines via winget ggml.llamacpp b9873, Vulkan backend pinned to the 7900 XTX (GGML_VK_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0). bitsandbytes INT8 is unavailable on this Windows-ROCm stack (Phase-0 gate MISS), so the quantized-verifier baseline is GGUF via llama.cpp rather than bnb-INT8.

llama_cpp.finding: Vanilla speculative decoding (0.6B draft -> 4B Q8_0) is ~0.96x on this hardware: draft overhead is not offset by acceptance on these prompts. This is the ordinary-speculative floor EdgeSpark's hidden-state-conditioned semi-AR drafter is meant to beat.

torch_verifier_fp16.stack: transformers 5.3.0 + PyTorch ROCm, Qwen3-4B fp16, attn=sdpa
torch_verifier_fp16.note: Per-op single-token forward latency at short context (GpuTimer / HIP events, synced). Faster than the llama.cpp sustained tg128 rate because it excludes sampling and long-context KV growth; used as the internal per-op constant for bench/simulate.py, not as the headline baseline.
